title: Vajrai Waterfall
tagline: Vajrai Waterfall — heritage city of Maharashtra

overview.short: The Bhambavli Vajrai Waterfall is a waterfall located on the Urmodi river in India.
overview.description: The Bhambavli Vajrai Waterfall is a waterfall located on the Urmodi river in India. It is approximately 27 kilometres (17 mi) from the city of Satara, Maharashtra, near the village of Bhambavli and Sahyadri Hill. The height of the waterfall combining all three tiers is 560 metres (1,840 ft).

topPlaces.name: Kas Plateau Reserved Forest
topPlaces.description: The Kas Plateau Reserved Forest, also known as the Kas Pathar, is a plateau situated 25 kilometres west of Satara city in Maharashtra, India. It falls under the Sahyadri Sub Cluster of the Western Ghats.

topPlaces.name: Vele, Satara
topPlaces.description: Vele is a village in the Western region of Maharashtra state in India. It is located in the Satara district of Maharashtra.

topPlaces.name: Jaoli
topPlaces.description: Jaoli is a tehsil, a taluka in subdivision of Satara district in the Indian state of Maharashtra.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Vajrai Waterfall?
faq.a: The best time to visit Vajrai Waterfall is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Vajrai Waterfall?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pune Airport (98 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (91 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Vajrai Waterfall?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Vajrai Waterfall at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Vajrai Waterfall?
faq.a: Stays in Vajrai Waterfall start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Vajrai Waterfall?
faq.a: The top attractions are Kas Plateau Reserved Forest, Vele, Satara, Jaoli.
